title: BestRAD Protocol for Genomic DNA Restriction Digest
author: Thom Nelson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6awhafe

steps.title: I
steps.instruction: I. Restriction Digest Dilute genomic DNA samples to a common concentration. For multiplexes of ≥48, 5 - 10 ng/ul will suffice. Add 10 µL (50-100 ng) of genomic DNA to each well of a 96-well PCR plate.

steps.title: Please select between the two following options
steps.instruction: Please select between the two following options: performing sheared, single-digest RAD performing double-digest

steps.title: Into 1
steps.instruction: Into 1.5 ml tube, make restriction digest master mix (vols for 100 rxns). 76.2 μL water 134.4 μL 10X NEBuffer 3.1 13.4 μL PstI (NEB R0140L)

steps.title: Add 76
steps.instruction: Add 76.2 µL, water .

steps.title: Add 134
steps.instruction: Add 134.4 µL, 10X NEBuffer 3.1 .

steps.title: Add 13
steps.instruction: Add 13.4 µL, PstI (NEB R0140L) .

steps.title: Pipette equal volumes of master mix...
steps.instruction: Pipette equal volumes of master mix into each tube of an 8-tube strip. ~ 27 µL ea for full 96-well plate.

steps.title: To each 96 plate well, pipet...
steps.instruction: To each 96 plate well, pipet 2 µL, PstI digestion master mix .

steps.title: In a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler: a) 37 °C for 01:00:00 . b) 80 °C for 00:20:00 . c) Slow ramp to 4 °C — 0.1 °C /sec

steps.title: For optimal enzyme activity and minimal...
steps.instruction: For optimal enzyme activity and minimal star activity, BfaI and PstI need to be used sequentially. PstI has lower activity and star activity in low-salt buffers like CutSmart, while BfaI has very reduced activity in high-salt buffers like NEB 3.1. Other enzyme combinations may be combined into a single digest step. Perform first digestion: BfaI master mix (add components to a 1.5 mL tube) For full 96-well plate: 76.2 µL, water 134.4 µL, 10X CutSmart Buffer 13.4 µL, BfaI (NEB R0568S) For 1/2 plate (48): 38.1 µL, water 67.2 µL, 10X CutSmart Buffer 6.7 µL, BfaI

steps.title: For full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For full 96-well plate: Add 76.2 µL, water . For 1/2 plate (48): Add 38.1 µL, water .

steps.title: For full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For full 96-well plate: Add 134.4 µL, 10X CutSmart Buffer . For 1/2 plate (48): Add 13.4 µL, BfaI (NEB R0568S) .

steps.title: For full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For full 96-well plate: Add 67.2 µL, 10X CutSmart Buffer . For 1/2 plate (48): Add 6.7 µL, BfaI .

steps.title: Pipette equal volumes of master mix...
steps.instruction: Pipette equal volumes of master mix into each tube of an 8-tube strip. ~ 27 µL ea for full 96-well plate.

steps.title: Pipette 2 µL, BfaI master mix into each well
steps.instruction: Pipette 2 µL, BfaI master mix into each well.

steps.title: Vortex plate to mix
steps.instruction: Vortex plate to mix.

steps.title: Quick spin to collect liquid
steps.instruction: Quick spin to collect liquid.

steps.title: In a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler: 37 °C for 01:00:00 . 80 °C for 00:20:00 . Slow ramp to 4 °C — 0.1 °C /sec On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, program available at THOM>CUTSMART.

steps.title: Perform second digestion
steps.instruction: Perform second digestion: PstI master mix (add components to a 1.5 mL tube) For a full 96-well plate 51.5 μL water 156.8 μL NEBuffer 3.1 15.7 μL PstI (NEB R0140L) For 48 reactions 25.8 μL water 78.4 μL NEBuffer 3.1 7.8 μL PstI

steps.title: For a full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For a full 96-well plate: Add 51.5 µL, water . For 48 reactions: Add 25.8 µL, water .

steps.title: For a full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For a full 96-well plate: Add 156.8 µL, NEBuffer 3.1 . For 48 reactions: Add 78.4 µL, NEBuffer 3.1 .

steps.title: For a full 96-well plate
steps.instruction: For a full 96-well plate: Add 15.7 µL, PstI (NEB R0140L) . For 48 reactions: Add 7.8 µL, PstI .

steps.title: Pipette equal volumes of master mix...
steps.instruction: Pipette equal volumes of master mix into each tube of an 8-tube strip. ~ 27 µL ea for full 96-well plate.

steps.title: Pipette 2 µL, PstI max mix into each well
steps.instruction: Pipette 2 µL, PstI max mix into each well.

steps.title: Vortex plate and quick spin
steps.instruction: Vortex plate and quick spin.

steps.title: In a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler: 37 °C for 01:00:00 . 80 °C for 00:20:00 . Slow ramp to 4 °C — 0.1 °C /sec On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, program available at THOM>CUTSMART.

steps.title: II
steps.instruction: II. Adaptor Ligation This part assumes 20 µL ligation volume and and use of NEB3.1 in the previous step. If only CutSmart buffer was used for digestion, supplement ligation buffer with fullstrength NEB2.1 ( 2 µL per well). Add 2 µL, annealed BestRad SbfI/PstI adaptors (50 nM) .

steps.title: Make ligation master mix (vols for 100 rxns)
steps.instruction: Make ligation master mix (vols for 100 rxns): 347.2 µL, water 56 µL, NEB2.1 22.4 µL, rATP (100 mM, Fermentas R0441) 22.4 µL, Ligase (NEB M0202L) From S. Bassham: NEB Buffer 2 is used in the ligation reactions in this protocol instead of ligase buffer because the salt it contains (50 mM NaCl) ensures the double-stranded adapters remain annealed during the reactions. T4 DNA Ligase is active in all 4 NEB Buffers if supplemented with 1mM rATP, but doesn’t work at maximum efficiency in NEB 3.

steps.title: Add 347
steps.instruction: Add 347.2 µL, water .

steps.title: Add 56 µL, NEB2
steps.instruction: Add 56 µL, NEB2.1 .

steps.title: Add 22
steps.instruction: Add 22.4 µL, rATP (100 mM, Fermentas R0441) .

steps.title: Add 22
steps.instruction: Add 22.4 µL, Ligase (NEB M0202L) .

steps.title: Pipette 56 µL, master mix into...
steps.instruction: Pipette 56 µL, master mix into each tube of an 8-tube strip for multichannel distribution.

steps.title: Into each 96 plate well, pipet...
steps.instruction: Into each 96 plate well, pipet 4 µL, ligation master mix .

steps.title: Incubate plate at 20 °C overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate plate at 20 °C overnight.

steps.title: III
steps.instruction: III. NEXT DAY Heat kill the ligation Kill the ligation reactions by deactivating ligase at 65 °C . In thermal cycler, incubate plate at 65 °C for 00:20:00 . On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, program available at THOM>LIGKILL.

steps.title: Kill the ligation reactions by deactivating ligase at 65 °C
steps.instruction: Kill the ligation reactions by deactivating ligase at 65 °C .

steps.title: In thermal cycler, incubate plate at 65 °C for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: In thermal cycler, incubate plate at 65 °C for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Using an 8-channel pipettor
steps.instruction: Using an 8-channel pipettor: a) transfer 10 µL of each adaptor-ligated sample into an 8-tube PCR strip.

steps.title: Combine pooled samples into a single 1
steps.instruction: Combine pooled samples into a single 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Split the multiplex into two or more aliquots
steps.instruction: Split the multiplex into two or more aliquots. a) The following steps assume 200 µL, multiplexed DNA .

steps.title: Store plate at -20 °C for future multiplexing
steps.instruction: Store plate at -20 °C for future multiplexing.

steps.title: Concentrate sample to 210 μL with Ampure 1X
steps.instruction: Concentrate sample to 210 μL with Ampure 1X. Divide sample into two Bioruptor tubes.

steps.title: Run 2 µL on the fragment...
steps.instruction: Run 2 µL on the fragment analyzer NGS mode to assay shearing efficiency.

steps.title: Sonicate with BioRuptor NGS
steps.instruction: Sonicate with BioRuptor NGS: 9 cycles: 00:00:30, on , 00:01:30, off

steps.title: I
steps.instruction: I. Before beginning the following steps, set a wet or dry bath to 56 °C .

steps.title: II
steps.instruction: II. Prepare Dynabead M280 streptavidin magnetic beads. Adjust bead volume.

steps.title: 2X Binding and Wash (B+W) Buffer
steps.instruction: 2X Binding and Wash (B+W) Buffer. 10 mM, TrisHCl (pH 7.5) 1 mM, EDTA pH 8.0 2 M, NaCl

steps.title: Transfer 30 µL, Dynabeads to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 30 µL, Dynabeads to a new 1.7 ml tube.

steps.title: Place tube in magnetic rack and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Place tube in magnetic rack and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Wash the beads (1/2) 100 µL,...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads (1/2) 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer Mix 00:00:30 Quick spin Remove supernate

steps.title: Add 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer .

steps.title: Mix for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Mix for 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Quick spin
steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Remove supernate.

steps.title: Wash the beads (2/2) 100 µL,...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads (2/2) 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer Mix 00:00:30 Quick spin Remove supernate

steps.title: Add 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL, 2X B+W buffer .

steps.title: Mix for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Mix for 00:00:30 .

steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Remove supernate.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in X μL 2X...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in X μL 2X B+W buffer (X = multiplex volume).

steps.title: III
steps.instruction: III. Bead Binding Add resuspended beads to ~ 200 µL, multiplexed DNA .

steps.title: While DNA fragments are binding, make...
steps.instruction: While DNA fragments are binding, make a dilution BW buffer 2-fold in 10 mM Tris. 1. Will need ~ 1 mL per multiplex 2. Split dilution into 2x 1.5mL tubes and set one tube in a 56 °C wet or dry bath.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00 . Mix every 00:02:00 by inverting.

steps.title: Quick spin Remove liquid from cap...
steps.instruction: Quick spin Remove liquid from cap of tube Not enough to pellet beads

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (1/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (1/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (2/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (2/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (3/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (3/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 56 °C 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (4/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (4/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 56 °C 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: IV
steps.instruction: IV. Liberate DNA from beads Dilute an aliquot of appropriate NEBuffer 10-fold to 1X.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 100 µL, 1X...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 100 µL, 1X restriction digest buffer (NEBuffer 3.1 for PstI ).

steps.title: Place on magnetic rack, remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack, remove supernate.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 40 µL, 1X digest buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 40 µL, 1X digest buffer .

steps.title: Transfer to PCR tube
steps.instruction: Transfer to PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 2 µL, PstI
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL, PstI .

steps.title: Incubate tube at 37 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate tube at 37 °C for 01:00:00 . On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, program available at THOM>CUTSMART. Because the next step removes the enzyme, there is no need to heat inactivate.

steps.title: Quick spin
steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack, KEEP SUPERNATE
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack, KEEP SUPERNATE!!

steps.title: V
steps.instruction: V. Bead cleanup of digestion reaction Allow an aliquot of Ampure XP beads to warm to Room temperature for ~ 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Vortex prior to addition to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex prior to addition to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 40 µL, Ampure XP beads to ligation reaction
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL, Ampure XP beads to ligation reaction. 1. Mix very well by pipetting a) slowly b) expel final volume slowly due to viscosity 2. Allow to incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 40 µL, Ampure XP beads to ligation reaction
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL, Ampure XP beads to ligation reaction.

steps.title: Mix very well by pipetting
steps.instruction: Mix very well by pipetting. slowly expel final volume slowly due to viscosity

steps.title: Allow to incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Allow to incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Make fresh 80% ethanol
steps.instruction: Make fresh 80% ethanol. ≥ 1 mL per reaction. Use aliquoted ethanol in 50 ml conical tubes to limit evaporation/hydration of ethanol.

steps.title: Place on magnetic rack and allow beads to pellet
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack and allow beads to pellet.

steps.title: Slowly remove supernate
steps.instruction: Slowly remove supernate.

steps.title: Ethanol wash (1/2) Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol
steps.instruction: Ethanol wash (1/2) Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol . Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol .

steps.title: Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet.

steps.title: Ethanol wash (2/2) Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol
steps.instruction: Ethanol wash (2/2) Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol . Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 80% ethanol .

steps.title: Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:00:30 with beads still on magnet.

steps.title: Remove all ethanol and allow beads...
steps.instruction: Remove all ethanol and allow beads to dry ~ 00:05:00 with the lid open.

steps.title: Add 55 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl to bead pellet
steps.instruction: Add 55 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl to bead pellet.

steps.title: Wash buffer over beads and pipette to resuspend
steps.instruction: Wash buffer over beads and pipette to resuspend. a) Suspension will turn a pale, even brown.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Place tube on magnet to separate beads
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnet to separate beads.

steps.title: Pipette supernate and place in a clean PCR tube
steps.instruction: Pipette supernate and place in a clean PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 55 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl to bead pellet
steps.instruction: Add 55 µL, 10 mM Tris-HCl to bead pellet.

steps.title: Wash buffer over beads and pipette to resuspend
steps.instruction: Wash buffer over beads and pipette to resuspend. Suspension will turn a pale, even brown.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Place tube on magnet to separate beads
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnet to separate beads.

steps.title: Pipette supernate and place in a clean PCR tube

steps.title: VI
steps.instruction: VI. Now is an okay time to stop if need be. Store eluted DNA at -20 °C overnight.

steps.title: Use full reactions NEBNext Ultra DNA...
steps.instruction: Use full reactions NEBNext Ultra DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (NEB E7370S/L) with no modifications except A. Use 1:10 diluted adaptor. B. Especially if performing double-digest, use a molecular barcoded i5 adaptor (see Materials ) I. End-repair and A-tailing This step blunts single-stranded DNA ends and adds a single A overhang. To the liberated DNA, add: 1. 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 2. 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer

steps.title: Add 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix to the liberated DNA.
. Mix by pipetting 10x w/ a pipette set to 50 µl.

steps.title: Add 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II...
steps.instruction: Add 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer to the liberated DNA.

steps.title: In a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler: 00:30:00 at 20 °C 00:30:00 at 65 °C Hold at 4 °C On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, use THOM>NEB_EP.

steps.title: II
steps.instruction: II. Adaptor ligation 1. This part adds universal Illumina adaptors onto all end-prepped fragments. NEB NEXT adaptors form hairpins and contain a single uracil in the loop. The USER enzyme mix has endonuclease activity that cleaves the adaptor at the U, creating a Y-shaped adaptor for subsequent addition of oligos and amplification by PCR. 2. From NEB: “If input DNA ≤ 100 ng, use 1:10 diluted adaptor (diluted in 10 mM Tris, 10 mM NaCl)”. Efficient RAD preps will isolate ~1%-15% of genomic DNA. Good luck getting anything greater than 100 ng total from 48 samples! Make sure Ligation Master Mix is well-mixed prior to addition. Vortex quickly, follow with quick spin.

steps.instruction: Vortex quickly.

steps.title: Quick spin
steps.instruction: Quick spin.

steps.title: 30 µL, NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix
steps.instruction: 30 µL, NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix

steps.title: 1 µL, NEBNext Ligation Enhancer
steps.instruction: 1 µL, NEBNext Ligation Enhancer
2.5 µL, NEBNext Adaptor for Illumina (diluted 1:10 from stock)

steps.title: Add 30 µL, NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix .

steps.title: Add 1 µL, NEBNext Ligation Enhancer
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL, NEBNext Ligation Enhancer .

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 µL, NEBNext Adaptor for Illumina (diluted 1:10 from stock) .

steps.title: IV
steps.instruction: IV. PCR enrichment of adaptor-ligated fragments To the purified DNA, add: 25 µL, NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 5 µL, i7 index primer 5 µL, i5 universal primer — USE i5 WITH MOLECULAR BARCODE

steps.title: Add 25 µL, NEBNext Ultra II...

steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, i5 universal primer to the purified DNA. — USE i5 WITH MOLECULAR BARCODE

steps.title: 98 °C for 00:00:30 — Denaturation Cycle

steps.title: VI
steps.instruction: VI. (Optional) Size selection of library A. Inefficient digestion or shearing of genomic DNA can result in large fragments (≥ 1000 bp) making it through library prep. B. In addition, size selection of double-digest RAD libraries can allow for customization of genomic coverage because all fragments originating from a specific RAD locus should be equal in length across samples but different from other RAD loci. C. Use agarose gel or Blue Pippin to size select libraries. At the very least clipping out any fragments greater than 1000 bp.

steps.title: I
steps.instruction: I. Before beginning the following steps, set a wet or dry bath to 56 °C .

steps.instruction: II. Prepare Dynabead M280 streptavidin magnetic beads. Adjust bead volume.

steps.instruction: 2X Binding and Wash (B+W) Buffer. 10 mM, TrisHCl (pH 7.5) 1 mM, EDTA pH 8.0 2 M, NaCl

steps.title: III
steps.instruction: III. Bead Binding Add resuspended beads to ~ 200 µL, multiplexed DNA .

steps.title: While DNA fragments are binding, make...
steps.instruction: While DNA fragments are binding, make a dilution BW buffer 2-fold in 10 mM Tris. 1. Will need ~ 1 mL per multiplex 2. Split dilution into 2x 1.5mL tubes and set one tube in a 56 °C wet or dry bath.

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 00:20:00 . Mix every 00:02:00 by inverting.

steps.title: Quick spin Remove liquid from cap...
steps.instruction: Quick spin Remove liquid from cap of tube Not enough to pellet beads

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (1/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (1/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (2/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (2/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (3/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 56 °C 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (4/4) Remove supernate
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack - Wash (4/4) Remove supernate. Resuspend beads in 56 °C 150 µL, 1X B+W Buffer .

steps.title: IV
steps.instruction: IV. Liberate DNA from beads Dilute an aliquot of appropriate NEBuffer 10-fold to 1X.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 100 µL, 1X...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 100 µL, 1X restriction digest buffer (NEBuffer 3.1 for PstI ).

steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack, remove supernate.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 40 µL, 1X digest buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 40 µL, 1X digest buffer .

steps.title: Transfer to PCR tube
steps.instruction: Transfer to PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 2 µL, PstI
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL, PstI .

steps.title: Incubate tube at 37 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate tube at 37 °C for 01:00:00 . On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, program available at THOM>CUTSMART. Because the next step removes the enzyme, there is no need to heat inactivate.

steps.title: Quick spin

steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack, KEEP SUPERNATE!!

steps.title: VI
steps.instruction: VI. Now is an okay time to stop if need be. Store eluted DNA at -20 °C overnight.

steps.title: Use full reactions NEBNext Ultra DNA...
steps.instruction: Use full reactions NEBNext Ultra DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (NEB E7370S/L) with no modifications except A. Use 1:10 diluted adaptor. B. Especially if performing double-digest, use a molecular barcoded i5 adaptor (see Materials ) I. End-repair and A-tailing This step blunts single-stranded DNA ends and adds a single A overhang. To the liberated DNA, add: 1. 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 2. 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer

steps.title: Add 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix to the liberated DNA.

steps.title: Add 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II...
steps.instruction: Add 7 µL, NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer to the liberated DNA.
. Mix by pipetting 10x w/ a pipette set to 50 µl.

steps.title: In a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler: 00:30:00 at 20 °C 00:30:00 at 65 °C Hold at 4 °C On Fishman Lab thermal cyclers, use THOM>NEB_EP.

steps.title: II
steps.instruction: II. Adaptor ligation 1. This part adds universal Illumina adaptors onto all end-prepped fragments. NEB NEXT adaptors form hairpins and contain a single uracil in the loop. The USER enzyme mix has endonuclease activity that cleaves the adaptor at the U, creating a Y-shaped adaptor for subsequent addition of oligos and amplification by PCR. 2. From NEB: “If input DNA ≤ 100 ng, use 1:10 diluted adaptor (diluted in 10 mM Tris, 10 mM NaCl)”. Efficient RAD preps will isolate ~1%-15% of genomic DNA. Good luck getting anything greater than 100 ng total from 48 samples! Make sure Ligation Master Mix is well-mixed prior to addition. Vortex quickly, follow with quick spin.
